our_open_deliverables.text: Send CLM demo presentation deck to SABIC attendees (Brittany Somerville, Brandon Lacey, Hassan Sheraz, Jorge, Ivana Harris, others) for internal review and discussion.

buyer_dependent_items.text: SABIC to provide availability (multiple time slots proposed by Zycus) for live SAP integration demo and confirm ERP/technical stakeholder attendance (Hassan, Steven, Jacco, Ivana, others).

SABIC to share list of external indices/sources (e.g., S&P, CMA, or other commodity/feedstock price benchmarks) used for price benchmarking to enable Zycus iContract price-benchmarking integration configuration.
SABIC to provide actual procurement data (invoice volumes, PO counts, cycle times, error rates) using Zycus data collection template to build customized ROI model.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of a formal top-5 field narrowing to two; the shortlist of direct P2P peers is undisclosed (highest competitive risk). Clearest displacement targets are incumbent SAP SRM (end-of-life — the compelling event) and SAP Ariba (Europe, being dropped). A third-party sourcing orchestration pilot lacks invoicing integration, which is Zycus's single-platform wedge. No specific named direct rival has surfaced, so none is asserted.

competitors.name: Undisclosed formal top-5 evaluation vendors
competitors.quote: We'll rank top 5 ... talk further with the top two ... and then make a decision.
competitors.how_we_win: Ask Joe directly who else is in the top five; once known, tailor differentiation, pricing and EB engagement to out-position the live peers before the down-select.

competitors.name: SAP SRM (incumbent being replaced)
competitors.quote: We run SAP SRM here. Of course, that's more or less end of life. We're looking for something to replace SRM.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the modern, easy-to-use SRM replacement; use the SAP SRM sunset as the compelling event to anchor the Nov timeline.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent, Europe sourcing/onboarding)
competitors.quote: For sourcing in Europe they use Ariba. They're gonna stop using it. We don't use it here in Americas.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast a single integrated S2P platform against fragmented Ariba; cite the painful, still-incomplete Ariba vendor re-onboarding to neutralize migration fear in the live SAP demo.

competitors.name: Third-party sourcing orchestration pilot (vendor unnamed)
competitors.quote: Pilot in progress covers source-to-contract activities such as RFP creation and bid analysis, but it lacks integration into invoicing and order-to-pay.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with sourcing-to-invoicing integration as the gap the pilot cannot close; position Zycus as the only single-platform answer rather than a two-tool stack.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery 06 May 2026; CLM demo 17 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: Joe Fischeco is a developing champion: he runs the evaluation and convenes procurement/IT/legal, but his advocacy is process-level and he has not opened a path to the economic buyer. The EB-introduction ask is the test that will reveal whether he is a true champion.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has appeared on any of the eight touchpoints. Joe Fischeco drives the evaluation but on 06 May described 'a combined effort ... people at my same role in Europe ... will be involved' — a committee above him Zycus has not met. Budgetary pricing was shared ~11 Jun and a public-data ROI on 29 Jun, yet there is no CFO/VP-Procurement meeting and no written approval path. For a $518k carve-out P2P decision this is the single largest risk and the reason Best Case is indefensible. The move is to have Joe name and broker the introduction to the Americas budget owner now — not to cold-outreach an unnamed EB, but to use the champion who authored the process as the path to power.

recommended_moves: Ask Joe to name and broker the introduction to the Americas carve-out budget owner — frame it as bringing the business case to the executive who approves the investment, ahead of the top-5 to top-2 down-select.
Collect SABIC's actual procurement data (invoice volumes, PO counts, cycle times) and build a SABIC-data ROI model to present to the budget owner — pairing it with the EB meeting.
Ask Joe directly who else is in the top five so positioning and pricing can be tailored before the top-2 down-select.
